column1: MOODLE COURSE STRUCTURE Ś DATA ENTRY REQUIREMENTS
Complete one row per activity. Work top to bottom within each module. Columns marked * are required.

column8: Activity Description

column4: How you think about money determines how you handle it. This module helps you understand your current money habits and build a healthier, more intentional relationship with your finances.
column8: Explore the beliefs and habits you grew up with about money. Identify which ones serve you well and which ones hold you back.
Track your actual spending for one week using a simple method. Most people are shocked to discover where their money really goes.

column7: The Difference Between Needs and Wants
column8: Learn a practical framework for separating genuine needs from emotional wants so you can make spending decisions you will not regret.

column4: A budget is not a restriction. It is a plan that gives you control. This module teaches you how to build and stick to a budget that works for your real life.
column8: Create a simple monthly budget using the 50/30/20 framework: needs, wants, and savings. Use your actual income and expenses.
Discover the practical strategies that help real people stick to their budget: cash envelopes, weekly check-ins, and spending limits by category.
Learn how to adapt your budget when your income changes, unexpected expenses arise, or life circumstances shift.

column3: Saving and Building an Emergency Fund
column4: Saving money is not about deprivation. It is about building options and security. Learn how to save consistently, even on a modest Ethiopian salary.
column8: Understand why even small, consistent savings create enormous long-term security. See the real impact of saving just 10% of your income over 5 years.
Learn the concept of an emergency fund: how much you need, where to keep it, and how to build it gradually without feeling deprived.
Learn to set specific savings goals (a phone, a course, a business idea) and create a plan to reach each one systematically.

column4: Debt can destroy financial freedom faster than anything else. This module teaches you how to borrow wisely, avoid predatory lending, and escape the debt cycle.
column7: Understanding Good Debt vs Bad Debt
column8: Learn the difference between borrowing to build (education, a business) and borrowing to consume (phones, clothes, lifestyle). One builds wealth; the other destroys it.
Identify the lending practices that trap young Ethiopians: informal loans with hidden terms, mobile lending apps with extreme interest, and social pressure to borrow.
If you already have debt, learn a step-by-step plan to pay it off: prioritise by interest rate, negotiate terms, cut expenses temporarily, and never borrow to repay a loan.

column4: Financial security is a marathon, not a sprint. This module introduces you to simple, accessible ways to grow your money and build wealth over the long term.
column8: Understand the basic concept of making your money work for you: savings accounts, equbs, bonds, and other accessible options for Ethiopian earners.
Learn why insurance and diversified income sources protect you from the unexpected: health emergencies, job loss, and family obligations.
Build a complete personal financial plan: your budget, your savings targets, your debt repayment plan, and your long-term wealth-building strategy.
